keyPoints: Definition of Public Key: A pair of keys, one public and one private, used for encryption and decryption.
Certificate of Public Keys: A document that verifies the authenticity of a public key.
Certificate Lifecycle: Issuance, Registration, and Revocation.
Public-Key Management Models: Hierarchical, Trust-Root, and Hybrid models.
Alternative Approaches: Web of Trust, OpenPGP, and Elliptic Curve Cryptography.
